senses.definition: a period of history identified with a particular event

examples.x: During the age of exploration, many new lands and trade routes were discovered.
The age of enlightenment brought about significant philosophical and scientific advancements.
An age of economic prosperity followed the industrial revolution, leading to increased wealth and development.
The age of digital media has revolutionized how we consume and share information.
The age of agriculture saw the development of farming techniques and settlement growth.

senses.definition: ‌a system that measures and divides the year into specified periods

examples.x: Different cultures may use various calendars, such as the lunar calendar or the Hebrew calendar, to mark time.
The fiscal calendar is used by businesses to manage financial reporting and budgeting.

senses.definition: to set a specific time to do something or make an event happen

examples.x: He scheduled his doctor's appointment for next week.
They scheduled the event for Saturday evening.
We scheduled the conference call for this afternoon.
She schedules her tasks for the week every Sunday.
The team is scheduling the project timeline.

senses.definition: a period of history marked by particular features or events

examples.x: The fall of the Berlin Wall marked the beginning of a new era in European politics.
The discovery of penicillin marked the start of a new era in medical treatment and antibiotics.
The Industrial Revolution ushered in an era of rapid technological and economic change.

senses.definition: a period consisting of two weeks or 14 days

examples.x: She planned her vacation to start in a fortnight, giving her time to prepare.
The project deadline was set for a fortnight from now to allow for thorough review.
They scheduled a follow-up meeting in a fortnight to assess progress on the proposal.
The event will be held in a fortnight, so guests should mark their calendars accordingly.

senses.definition: a period of one thousand years, usually calculated from the year of the birth of Jesus Christ

examples.x: Historians study events that occurred during the first millennium AD to understand ancient civilizations.
The invention of printing was a pivotal development during the second millennium.
The medieval cathedral took nearly a millennium to complete, showcasing exquisite craftsmanship.
Futurists speculate about technological advancements that may shape the next millennium.

senses.definition: a region of the earth that has the same standard time

examples.x: The conference call was scheduled to accommodate participants in different time zones.
He adjusted his watch when traveling to a different time zone to match the local time.
Time zones help coordinate global activities and ensure synchronized schedules.
The airline pilot announced the local time and current time zone upon landing.
Digital devices automatically update to the correct time zone based on their location using GPS technology.

senses.definition: used to indicate that something should have happened or been done earlier

examples.x: It's about time you start taking your health seriously and prioritize exercise.
She tells her friend, "It's about time we visit that new restaurant everyone's been talking about."
It was about time he realized the importance of saving money and started budgeting.
We finally went on that long-awaited vacation—it was about time we took a break.
Tomorrow, it will be about time to submit the project that we've been working on for months.
By next month, it will be about time for us to move into our new house.

senses.definition: the standard time measured in a specific region

examples.x: He set his watch to local time after arriving in the new country.
She called her friend and remembered to check the local time to avoid calling too early or too late.
They had to adjust their clocks to account for the local time when they traveled to a new time zone.
The flight departure time is listed as 10 AM local time for the airport's location.

senses.definition: a timepiece that shows the time in a very exact way, especially one used at sea

examples.x: The chronometer's accuracy was tested regularly to maintain its reliability for professional use.
The navigator relied on a marine chronometer to determine the ship's longitude during the voyage.
She used a high-quality chronometer to ensure accurate timing in her scientific experiments.
The chronometer was essential for astronomers to track celestial events with precision.
They calibrated the chronometer to ensure it met the strict standards for accuracy in their research.

senses.definition: a glass container with two parts that measures every sixty minutes using the sand flow from the upper to the lower part

examples.x: She flipped the hourglass to start the 30-minute countdown for her cooking task.
The hourglass on the desk was used as a timer for the study session.
They used an hourglass during the game to keep track of turns and ensure fairness.
The hourglass was a traditional method for measuring time before modern clocks became common.
He watched the sand slowly flow through the hourglass as he waited for his meeting to start.

senses.definition: a type of clock that works using a swinging weight at the end of a straight line

examples.x: The antique pendulum clock chimed every hour, marking the passage of time in the living room.
He inherited a grandfather pendulum clock that had been in the family for generations.
Pendulum clocks were widely used before the invention of quartz and atomic clocks.

senses.definition: a watch with a button to stop and start time, used in sport events

examples.x: The coach used a stopwatch to time each runner during the track practice.
She started the stopwatch as soon as the race began to measure her performance.
The scientist used a stopwatch to record the duration of a chemical reaction in the lab.
He reset the stopwatch after each trial to ensure accurate measurements in the experiment.
They used a digital stopwatch for precise timing in the competition.

senses.definition: an instrument used in the past to tell the time using the shadow made by a metal piece on a flat stone

examples.x: They restored an old sundial as a historical feature in the park’s new design.

senses.definition: the time in the evening when the sun is below the horizon

examples.x: The colors of the sky during twilight were breathtaking, blending shades of orange and purple.
They enjoyed a romantic walk along the beach during the tranquil twilight hours.
Twilight is often considered a magical time when the world transitions from day to night.
The photographer captured stunning silhouettes against the twilight sky.
Birds chirped softly as daylight faded into twilight, signaling the end of another day.

senses.definition: the fact or quality of arriving, happening, or being done after the usual or expected time

examples.x: Her lateness to the meeting was noted, and she apologized for the delay.
He was concerned about his lateness to the appointment due to heavy traffic.
They discussed the consequences of habitual lateness with their employees.
The train’s lateness caused inconvenience for many commuters trying to get to work on time.
She tried to make up for her lateness by working extra hours to finish the task.

senses.definition: organized according to the order that the events occurred in

examples.x: The historical events were presented in chronological order.
The biography was organized in chronological order, tracing the subject's life from birth to death.
The professor presented the material in the textbook in chronological order to help students understand the progression of events.
The museum exhibit showcased artifacts in chronological order, illustrating the development of civilization.

senses.definition: happening or made very quickly and easily

examples.x: The microwave oven offers instant heating for food.
The company offers instant access to online services for its customers.
The new software promises instant results with just a few clicks.

senses.definition: at the present time, with the understanding that the current situation or decision may be changed in the near future

examples.x: We have enough supplies for the moment, but we'll need to restock soon.
I'm focusing on this project for the moment; other tasks can wait.
For the moment, the issue seems to be resolved, but we'll keep monitoring it.
The team is content with the current plan for the moment, but revisions may be necessary.
I'll hold off on making a decision for the moment until I gather more information.

senses.definition: in the recent period of time

examples.x: I've been feeling tired lately.
Lately, I've been enjoying reading mystery novels.
She has been studying a lot lately.
Have you seen her around here lately?
The weather has been quite unpredictable lately.

senses.definition: taking place or done each day

examples.x: She manages the day-to-day operations of the office, ensuring everything runs smoothly.
The day-to-day responsibilities of a teacher include planning lessons and grading assignments.
He handles the day-to-day needs of his family, from cooking meals to scheduling appointments.
They discussed the day-to-day challenges of running a small business.
The day-to-day activities in the hospital include patient care and administrative tasks.

senses.definition: happening, done, or made once every year

examples.x: They celebrated their annual family reunion in the summer.
The company held its annual holiday party in December.
She received her annual performance review in January.
He attended the annual charity gala to support a good cause.
The school organized its annual sports day event in the fall.

senses.definition: in a way that happens once every year

examples.x: The insurance premium is paid annually.
She attends the conference annually.
The company hosts a charity event annually.
He reviews his investment portfolio annually.
The garden show takes place annually.

senses.definition: happening or done once every month

examples.x: Their monthly meetings allow the team to align on goals and address any challenges.
The magazine published its monthly issue on the first day of each month.
She made her monthly visit to the elderly home to volunteer.
They organized a monthly book club meeting on the second Tuesday of each month.

senses.definition: happening, done, or made every week

examples.x: They attended their weekly yoga class on Monday evenings.
The magazine published a weekly issue every Sunday.
He received his weekly paycheck on Fridays.
They held their weekly staff meeting every Monday morning.
She scheduled her weekly grocery shopping for Saturday mornings.

senses.definition: for a very short time

examples.x: He paused momentarily to catch his breath.
The internet connection dropped momentarily before reconnecting.
The train stopped momentarily for passengers to board.
The sun emerged from behind the clouds momentarily.
She hesitated momentarily before making a decision.

senses.definition: on occasions that are not regular or frequent

examples.x: They visit their hometown now and again.
He listens to jazz music now and again.
She changes her hairstyle now and again.
I like to go hiking now and again when the weather is nice.
Now and again, she visits her old hometown to see friends.

senses.definition: on irregular but not rare occasions

examples.x: She enjoys a dessert now and then.
We meet for lunch every now and then.
They take short vacations now and then.
She rearranges her furniture every now and then.
Every now and then, I like to watch old movies from my childhood.

senses.definition: for a longer period than normal
after the time mentioned or in the future

examples.x: I'll finish the task later on, after I take a break.
We can discuss the details later on in the meeting.
Later on, I plan to explore other career opportunities.
She'll provide additional information later on in the project.
Later on, we might consider expanding the business.
